channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Fallout from 2023 Hamas attack lingers as Jews worldwide prepare for a holiday meant for joy
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Customarily, Simchat Torah is one of the most joyous days of the Jewish calendar, highlighted by exuberant dancing around a Torah. Jewish leaders say the joy will return during this week's celebrations, even as Jews recall that last year's Simchat Torah overlapped with Oct.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump hurls string of insults at Harris including racist trope against Black people
item.description: Trump signaled he will lean harder into disparaging Harris during the remaining two weeks before Election Day, despite the urging of allies who have repeatedly suggested he should steer clear of personal attacks including references to her race and gender.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kuwait bans 'Call of Duty: Black Ops 6' video game, likely over it featuring Saddam Hussein in 1990s
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The tiny Mideast nation of Kuwait has banned the release of the video game “Call of Duty: Black Ops 6,” which features the late Iraqi dictator Saddam Hussein and is set in part in the 1990s Gulf War.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Storm-triggered floods in Philippines spark frantic calls to rescue trapped people, some on roofs
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Torrential rain set off by an approaching tropical storm swamped eastern Philippine cities and towns overnight in widespread flooding that trapped people, some on their roofs, and sparked frantic appeals for rescue boats and trucks, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: North Carolina CEO uses personal helicopter to save 11-day-old baby from remote area ravaged by Helene
item.description: Zeb Hadley, a private helicopter owner and CEO of National Coatings, ended up saving an 11-day-old baby who was born prematurely just before Hurricane Helene struck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look behind the scenes at the National Toy Hall of Fame
item.description: ROCHESTER, N.Y. (AP) — When curators at the National Toy Hall of Fame learned last fall that the Fisher-Price Corn Popper had been voted in as part of the class of 2023, they knew they had some serious work to do.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MS-13 gang member convicted for ‘gruesome murders' in Northern Virginia, Massachusetts
item.description: Elmer De Jesus Alas Candray faces life behind bars after being convicted of killing six people between 2018 and 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Capitol Christmas Tree is coming to Utah as part of its 4,000-mile trek -- Here's how it's staying alive
item.description: Here's what you should know about this year's Capitol Christmas Tree — and how the tree will be kept alive during its travels.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio man charged with bringing massive 'Trump' sign to Capitol for rioters to use as a weapon
item.description: An Ohio man was arrested Tuesday on charges that he brought a massive “Trump” sign to the U.S. Capitol and joined other rioters in using it as a weapon against police officers during a mob attack.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Court upholds freedom for woman whose conviction was overturned after 43 years behind bars
item.description: An appellate court in Missouri has ruled that a lower court was right when it decided to overturn the murder conviction of a woman who spent 43 years behind bars for a killing that her attorneys argue was committed by a discredited police officer.
item.dc:creator: Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Walmart ordered to pay millions for disposing of toxic, medical waste at local landfills
item.description: The world's most profitable retailer has been ordered to pay approximately $7.5 million in penalties for violating environmental regulations regarding the disposal of toxic and medical waste.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MLB wants the Tampa Bay Rays to play in the area if hurricane-damaged Tropicana Field is unfit
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Major League Baseball hopes to find the Tampa Bay Rays a temporary home near their fanbase at least for the start of the 2025 season if the damage to Tropicana Field from Hurricane Milton cannot be repaired in time. The translucent fiberglass dome at the ballpark in St.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Abercrombie & Fitch chief Mike Jeffries arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Mike Jeffries, his romantic partner and a third man were arrested Tuesday on charges of luring men into drug-laced, outlandish and coercive sex parties by dangling the promise of modeling for the retailer's once-defining beefcake ads.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina county surrounding Asheville overcounted Helene deaths by as many as 30, sheriff says
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The North Carolina county that is home to Asheville overcounted deaths caused by Helene by as many as 30, according to a statement Tuesday from its sheriff’s office and data from the state, significantly reducing the death toll from the historic storm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's Quarter Pounders linked to deadly E. coli outbreak
item.description: McDonald's Quarter Pounders sold at restaurants across the country may have been contaminated with E. coli, according to a warning from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. The CDC says at least 49 people have contracted E.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Severe E. coli outbreak linked to McDonald's quarter pounders
item.description: The CDC has issued a food safety alert for an outbreak of E. coli infections linked to McDonald's quarter pounders, with 49 people from ten states affected and one death reported.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, NewsNation

item.title: Body of missing SpaceX employee found at Texas beach
item.description: The body was recovered by Cameron County local authorities at Boca Chica Beach near the original point where he was lost shortly after 6:30 p.m. on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Salinas, NewsNation

item.title: Biden heaps praise on Slovenia PM for aiding release of Americans in major US-Russia prisoner swap
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden heaped praise on Slovenian Prime Minister Robert Golob on Tuesday for his role in the recent seven-nation prisoner swap that freed Americans Evan Gershkovich, Alsu Kurmasheva and Paul Whelan from Russian prison. The deal, completed in August, was the largest U.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rudy Giuliani ordered to turn over NYC apartment, 26 watches to Georgia election workers
item.description: A federal judge has ordered Rudy Giuliani to turn over his New York City apartment and various other assets to two Georgia election workers who won a $148 million defamation judgment against him.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Grieving families of seniors killed when a dock collapsed say the tragedy was preventable
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Regina Brinson heard a crack before the metal walkway gave way beneath her feet, plunging her into the water beneath the state-operated ferry dock on Georgia's Sapelo Island.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Richard N. Winfield, First Amendment lawyer and former AP counsel, dies at 91
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Richard N. Winfield, a leading U.S. First Amendment lawyer who represented The Associated Press for three decades and championed freedom of expression for journalists around the world, died on Tuesday. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Georgia teen accused in the Apalachee High School shooting has pleaded not guilty
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A 14-year-old Georgia boy indicted on murder charges in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School pleaded not guilty on Tuesday. Colt Gray's lawyer filed papers entering the plea after Gray was indicted on Thursday. They waived an arraignment hearing that had been scheduled for Nov.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders Giuliani to hand over assets to Georgia election workers
item.description: Rudy Giuliani must turn over much of his property — his apartment in New York, the city that made him mayor, and other prized possessions — to two Georgia election workers who have sought for months to collect their massive defamation judgment, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: Asheville annual holiday tradition canceled due to Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (WSPA) - An annual national holiday tradition that has taken place for over three decades in Asheville has been canceled due to the aftermath of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, Fox 5

item.title: On Lake Erie, getting rid of problem algae starts with giving it less food
item.description: OREGON, Ohio (AP) — On a warm late-summer evening, a small speedboat motored across a pea-green stretch of Lake Erie past a beach where a child sat splashing and a pair of newlyweds waded for a portrait photographer.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA A. BICKEL and PATRICK AFTOORA-ORSAGOS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helicopter crashes into radio tower in Houston, killing 4
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Four people aboard a helicopter were killed when it crashed into a radio tower in Houston and burst into flames, officials said. The helicopter went down just before 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Central Park Five sue Trump for defamation over debate comments
item.description: The exonerated Central Park Five sued former President Donald Trump for defamation on Monday over his comments at the recent presidential debate about their wrongful convictions for rape and assault.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: What is pink cocaine? Autopsy finds drug in Liam Payne's body
item.description: A partial autopsy conducted on former One Direction singer Liam Payne found multiple substances in his system when he fell to his death from the third-floor balcony of a hotel room in Argentina last week, according to a new report from ABC News.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, Fox 5

item.title: Yankees and Dodgers meet in World Series for 12th time in matchup of Broadway and Hollywood
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Broadway vs. Hollywood. Subway vs. Freeway. Judge vs. Ohtani. New York neighbors who became cross-country rivals, the Yankees and Dodgers renew their starry struggle in the World Series for the first time in 43 years.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Who is Lucifer 'Justin Case' Everylove? Meet the presidential candidate who is only on Utah's ballots
item.description: Who is Lucifer "Justin Case" Everylove? Here's how this unaffiliated presidential candidate made it onto the ballots in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KRON4

item.title: Helicopter crash in Houston kills 4, topples radio tower, officials say
item.description: The private helicopter with four people on board, including a child, either struck a cable or the tower, Houston Police Chief J. Noe Diaz said at a news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris raises more than $220 million in September, outraising Trump 3-to-1
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris’s campaign raised nearly $222 million in September, raising more than three times as much as former President Donald Trump’s campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: This $600 robot litter box is life changing
item.description: Cats are great companions, but scooping out their litter box isn't exactly a chore you look forward to. I've long been curious about automated litter boxes that promise to do the dirty work.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Over 1 million voters cast early ballots in North Carolina
item.description: Nearly 1.01 million North Carolinians have already voted in the November general election, state officials reported on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, ABC 4

item.title: 2 Navy aviators are declared dead after a fighter jet crashed in Washington state
item.description: Two crew members who were missing following the crash of a fighter jet in mountainous terrain in Washington state during a routine training flight have been declared dead, the U.S. Navy said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kentucky family demands answers after organs nearly taken from living man
item.description: A Kentucky family is looking for answers after a man was declared brain dead and being prepared to have his organs harvested while he was still alive.
item.dc:creator: Kiley Hill, WGN 9
